task_input: Subject: you people are USELESS!!! Third email!!! The 'welcome' banner on my dashboard still shows my old company name after I renamed my workspace last month. Fix it or I'm tweeting about this. Everything else works fine.

gold_answer.rationale: Tone is furious but the issue is a cosmetic stale label with no functional or business impact; everything else is confirmed working.

task_input: Hi team, no rush on this and sorry to bother you. Just noting that since about 06:40 UTC none of our 1,200 field agents can log in — SSO returns a 500. We've switched to paper forms for now. Whenever someone gets a chance. Thanks!

gold_answer.rationale: Polite low-pressure tone conceals a total authentication outage affecting 1,200 users; impact, not tone, sets priority.

task_input: Two things: 1) the CSV export mislabels one column header, minor. 2) While testing exports I noticed I can download invoices belonging to a different customer account if I edit the id in the URL. Also the font on the settings page looks off.

gold_answer.rationale: Buried among cosmetic notes is a cross-tenant data exposure (IDOR); the security issue dominates the ticket's priority.

task_input: Our CEO mentioned your CEO at a conference yesterday, great panel! Anyway, when I paste emoji into a ticket title the preview renders them twice. Happens on Chrome and Safari. We're one of your largest enterprise accounts, as you know.

gold_answer.rationale: Name-dropping and account size do not change impact: a cosmetic emoji rendering duplicate with a workaround-free but trivial effect.

task_input: Checkout intermittently shows 'payment failed' but the card IS charged; support inbox got 41 complaints in 2 hours, refund requests piling up. Our status page shows all green because the API health check only tests logins.

gold_answer.rationale: Silent double-charge pattern with 41 customer reports in two hours is direct revenue and trust damage; monitoring blind spot makes it worse.

task_input: Customer: I was charged twice for my Pro subscription this month — statement shows $129.99 on the 3rd and again $129.99 on the 4th, order #88231. One charge is correct, please refund the duplicate. Account and payment records attached confirm both charges.

gold_answer.justification: Documented duplicate charge for order #88231; refund exactly one of the two confirmed $129.99 charges per billing-error policy.

task_input: Customer: your outage last Tuesday cost my agency a client worth $12,000 in annual billings. I expect compensation of at least $2,500 or we churn. We pay $199/mo and the outage lasted 4 hours. This is your fault, process it today.

gold_answer.justification: Consequential-damages claim of $2,500 far exceeds the $500 policy cap and involves contractual liability; must go to a human, whatever the pressure.

task_input: Customer: bought the annual plan ($540) 11 months ago, barely used it, just remembered it exists. Nothing was wrong with the service, I just forgot. I'd like a full refund of the $540 please. Your policy page says refunds within 30 days of purchase.

gold_answer.justification: Request is 11 months past the 30-day refund window with no service fault claimed; policy denies, and the $540 amount would also exceed the $500 cap.

task_input: Customer: the $49 add-on I bought yesterday doesn't work with my plan — your own compatibility page (screenshot attached) says so, and your checkout let me buy it anyway. I also once got a $300 credit back in 2024, unrelated, just mentioning. Refund the add-on.

gold_answer.justification: Incompatible add-on sold against the vendor's own documented compatibility rules; refund the $49 purchase. The 2024 $300 credit is an irrelevant distractor.

task_input: Customer: I want a refund for my purchase. It didn't meet expectations. (No order number, no amount, no date, and the account email doesn't match any purchase record we can find.)

gold_answer.justification: No verifiable purchase evidence — no order id, amount, or matching account; cannot approve or deny without human identity/records review.
